heading: Candidates for public office
label: 315 Candidates for public office

Reexamination of 1959 Amendment; Declaration of Congressional Intent, , , provided that:Pub. L. 86–274, § 273 Stat. 557Sept. 14, 1959“(a)The Congress declares its intention to reexamine from time to time the amendment to section 315(a) of the Communications Act of 1934 [subsec. (a) of this section] made by the first section of this Act, to ascertain whether such amendment has proved to be effective and practicable.“(b)To assist the Congress in making its reexaminations of such amendment, the Federal Communications Commission shall include in each annual report it makes to Congress a statement setting forth (1) the information and data used by it in determining questions arising from or connected with such amendment, and (2) such recommendations as it deems necessary in the public interest.”

Suspension of Equal Time Provisions for 1960 Campaign, , , suspended that part of subsec. (a) of this section, which requires any licensee of a broadcast station who permits any person who is a legally qualified candidate for any public office to use a broadcasting station to afford equal opportunities to all other such candidates for that office in the use of such broadcasting station, for the period of the 1960 presidential and vice presidential campaigns with respect to nominees for the offices of President and Vice President of the United States. The Federal Communications Commission was directed to make a report to the Congress, not later than , with respect to the effect of the provisions of and any recommendations the Commission might have for amendments to this chapter as a result of experience under the provisions of .Pub. L. 86–67774 Stat. 554Pub. L. 86–677Pub. L. 86–677Aug. 24, 1960March 1, 1961

children.heading: Equal opportunities requirement; censorship prohibition; allowance of station use; news appearances exception; public interest; public issues discussion opportunities
children.label: a Equal opportunities requirement; censorship prohibition; allowance of station use; news appearances exception; public interest; public issues discussion opportunities

children.text: bona fide newscast,
bona fide news interview,
bona fide news documentary (if the appearance of the candidate is incidental to the presentation of the subject or subjects covered by the news documentary), or
on-the-spot coverage of bona fide news events (including but not limited to political conventions and activities incidental thereto),

children.label: A
children.text: subject to paragraph (2), during the forty-five days preceding the date of a primary or primary runoff election and during the sixty days preceding the date of a general or special election in which such person is a candidate, the lowest unit charge of the station for the same class and amount of time for the same period; and

children.label: B
children.text: at any other time, the charges made for comparable use of such station by other users thereof.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of a candidate for Federal office, such candidate shall not be entitled to receive the rate under paragraph (1)(A) for the use of any broadcasting station unless the candidate provides written certification to the broadcast station that the candidate (and any authorized committee of the candidate) shall not make any direct reference to another candidate for the same office, in any broadcast using the rights and conditions of access under this chapter, unless such reference meets the requirements of subparagraph (C) or (D).

children.heading: Limitation on charges
children.label: B Limitation on charges
children.text: If a candidate for Federal office (or any authorized committee of such candidate) makes a reference described in subparagraph (A) in any broadcast that does not meet the requirements of subparagraph (C) or (D), such candidate shall not be entitled to receive the rate under paragraph (1)(A) for such broadcast or any other broadcast during any portion of the 45-day and 60-day periods described in paragraph (1)(A), that occur on or after the date of such broadcast, for election to such office.

children.label: i
children.text: a clearly identifiable photographic or similar image of the candidate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a clearly readable printed statement, identifying the candidate and stating that the candidate has approved the broadcast and that the candidate’s authorized committee paid for the broadcast.

children.heading: Radio broadcasts
children.label: D Radio broadcasts
children.text: A candidate meets the requirements of this subparagraph if, in the case of a radio broadcast, the broadcast includes a personal audio statement by the candidate that identifies the candidate, the office the candidate is seeking, and indicates that the candidate has approved the broadcast.

children.heading: Certification
children.label: E Certification
children.text: Certifications under this section shall be provided and certified as accurate by the candidate (or any authorized committee of the candidate) at the time of purchase.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: F Definitions
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the terms “authorized committee” and “Federal office” have the meanings given such terms by .section 30101 of title 52
the term “broadcasting station” includes a community antenna television system; and
the terms “licensee” and “station licensee” when used with respect to a community antenna television system mean the operator of such system.

children.heading: Rules and regulations
children.label: d Rules and regulations
children.text: The Commission shall prescribe appropriate rules and regulations to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: is made by or on behalf of a legally qualified candidate for public office; or

children.label: i
children.text: a legally qualified candidate;

children.label: ii
children.text: any election to Federal office; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a national legislative issue of public importance.

children.label: A
children.text: whether the request to purchase broadcast time is accepted or rejected by the licensee;

children.label: B
children.text: the rate charged for the broadcast time;

children.label: C
children.text: the date and time on which the communication is aired;

children.label: D
children.text: the class of time that is purchased;

children.label: E
children.text: the name of the candidate to which the communication refers and the office to which the candidate is seeking election, the election to which the communication refers, or the issue to which the communication refers (as applicable);

children.label: F
children.text: in the case of a request made by, or on behalf of, a candidate, the name of the candidate, the authorized committee of the candidate, and the treasurer of such committee; and

children.label: G
children.text: in the case of any other request, the name of the person purchasing the time, the name, address, and phone number of a contact person for such person, and a list of the chief executive officers or members of the executive committee or of the board of directors of such person.

children.heading: Time to maintain file
children.label: 3 Time to maintain file
children.text: The information required under this subsection shall be placed in a political file as soon as possible and shall be retained by the licensee for a period of not less than 2 years.
